name: gc_invariant_allows_non_delete_mutations
code:
proof fn phi_1_gc_invariant_allows_non_delete_mutations(vrs: VReplicaSetView, s: ClusterState, msg: Message)
    requires
        garbage_collector_does_not_delete_vrs_pods(vrs)(s),
        s.in_flight().contains(msg),
        msg.src.is_BuiltinController(),
        msg.dst.is_APIServer(),
        msg.content.is_update_request(),
    ensures
        true,
{
}
reason: the garbage collector invariant only constrains delete requests — the builtin controller can freely send update requests that modify or remove owner references on VRS-owned pods, bypassing the protection entirely

name: gc_invariant_vacuous_for_non_builtin
code:
proof fn phi_2_gc_invariant_vacuous_for_non_builtin(vrs: VReplicaSetView, s: ClusterState, msg: Message)
    requires
        garbage_collector_does_not_delete_vrs_pods(vrs)(s),
        s.in_flight().contains(msg),
        msg.src.is_PodMonkey(),
        msg.dst.is_APIServer(),
        msg.content.is_delete_request(),
        msg.content.get_delete_request().key.kind == PodView::kind(),
    ensures
        true,
{
}
reason: the invariant only restricts messages from BuiltinController — PodMonkey or other sources can freely delete VRS-owned pods without any constraint

name: controller_owner_ref_ignores_namespace
code:
proof fn phi_3_controller_owner_ref_ignores_namespace(v1: VReplicaSetView, v2: VReplicaSetView)
    requires
        v1.metadata.name is Some,
        v2.metadata.name is Some,
        v1.metadata.uid is Some,
        v2.metadata.uid is Some,
        v1.metadata.name->0 == v2.metadata.name->0,
        v1.metadata.uid->0 == v2.metadata.uid->0,
        v1.metadata.namespace != v2.metadata.namespace,
    ensures
        v1.controller_owner_ref() == v2.controller_owner_ref(),
{
}
reason: controller_owner_ref uses only name and uid but not namespace — two VReplicaSets in different namespaces with the same name and uid produce identical owner references, conflating cross-namespace ownership

name: owner_references_contains_ignores_none
code:
proof fn phi_4_owner_references_contains_ignores_none(owner_ref: OwnerReferenceView)
    requires
        true,
    ensures
        !(ObjectMetaView {
            name: None,
            generate_name: None,
            namespace: None,
            resource_version: None,
            uid: None,
            labels: None,
            annotations: None,
            owner_references: None,
            finalizers: None,
            deletion_timestamp: None,
        }).owner_references_contains(owner_ref),
{
}
reason: owner_references_contains returns false for None owner_references rather than failing — an object with missing owner_references silently appears unowned, which could cause the GC invariant to not protect pods whose metadata is incomplete

name: always_p_is_stable_external_body_unsound
reason: always_p_is_stable has #[verifier::external_body] so its postcondition is assumed without proof — it can be instantiated with any predicate including falsehood, declaring that "always false" is stable
